title: Public datasets for individual electricity load forecasting
text: vignette gives short overview several open datasets useful benchmark methods implemented elcf4R package forecasting individual small group electricity load curves. focuses datasets provide relatively high temporal resolution can linked local weather conditions, especially outdoor temperature. redistribute original datasets inside package license compatible redistribution CRAN. Instead, provide small synthetic heavily downsampled examples helper functions download original data directly authors repositories allowed. main datasets covered : ELMAS: hourly commercial industrial profiles France. StoreNet Ireland: energy community dataset Ireland. IDEAL: detailed electricity, gas contextual data UK homes. GX-residential: transformer level residential loads Guangxi, China. also briefly mention datasets might interest specific applications.

title: Public datasets for individual electricity load forecasting
text: ELMAS dataset collection 18 typical hourly load profiles derived 55,730 French industrial tertiary customers, aggregated 424 fields activity three subscribed power levels one year. released Bellinguer et al. Scientific Data (2023) figshare collection. Resolution: hourly. Spatial level: load profiles per business segment, individual meters. Time span: 1 year. Weather: included, profiles can joined external weather data regional level. License: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 (CC 4.0) time writing, allows redistribution proper attribution. full dataset can downloaded figshare. R can use: elcf4R package provides small subset profiles dataset elcf4r_elmas_demo, restricted activity sectors shorter time window. subset stored inside package CC 4.0 compatible license explicit attribution DESCRIPTION help page dataset.
code: elmas_url <- "https://figshare.com/ndownloader/files/41479693" dest <- tempfile(fileext = ".zip") utils::download.file(elmas_url, destfile = dest, mode = "wb") unzip(dest, exdir = tempdir())

title: Public datasets for individual electricity load forecasting
text: Trivedi et al. (2024) describe comprehensive dataset electrical load profiles energy community Ireland. dataset contains per household power energy measurements, photovoltaic production, battery state charge, grid imports exports, local weather variables temperature, wind speed solar irradiance. Resolution: 1 minute power, 15 minutes energy quantities. Spatial level: individual households within energy community. Time span: multiple years. Weather: included community level. License: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 (CC 4.0) data repository time writing. authors host data code public repository linked Scientific Data article. typical way download data R : exact URL can change, helper function elcf4r_download_storenet() shipped package queries figshare API returns local path extracted files. See documentation details. original dataset large, elcf4R ships small subsample named elcf4r_storenet_demo containing households weeks data temperature.
code: storenet_zip <- "https://figshare.com/ndownloader/files/45123456" dest <- tempfile(fileext = ".zip") utils::download.file(storenet_zip, destfile = dest, mode = "wb") unzip(dest, exdir = tempdir())

title: Public datasets for individual electricity load forecasting
text: IDEAL dataset contains whole house appliance level electricity data, gas consumption contextual sensor readings 255 homes United Kingdom collected 23 months, typical resolutions 1 second aggregate electricity 12 seconds temperature, humidity light. Sensor data complemented detailed survey building metadata. Resolution: 1 second aggregate electricity, 12 seconds sensors. Spatial level: individual homes, appliance level detail. Time span: 23 months per home. Weather: directly given, households concentrated around Edinburgh external weather data can joined. License: Creative Commons Attribution Non Commercial 4.0 (CC NC 4.0). non commercial clause, original data redistributed inside elcf4R. Instead, provide helper function elcf4r_download_ideal() downloads data Edinburgh DataShare repository user accepted licence terms web browser. Example: examples tests, rely small synthetic dataset elcf4r_synth mimics temporal structure single household reuse IDEAL measurements.
code: path_ideal <- elcf4r_download_ideal() list.files(path_ideal, recursive = TRUE)[1:20]

title: Public datasets for individual electricity load forecasting
text: Li et al. (2025) release hourly transformer level residential consumption data 23 communities Guangxi Province, China, covering 2022 2023. dataset includes meteorological variables temperature humidity flags extreme weather events holidays. Resolution: hourly. Spatial level: transformer level aggregated demand. Time span: two years. Weather: multiple meteorological variables calendar indicators. License: Creative Commons Attribution Non Commercial Derivatives 4.0 (CC NC ND 4.0). Due non commercial derivatives restrictions, elcf4R redistribute part dataset. provide convenience function elcf4r_download_gx() retrieves original archive figshare location associated GitHub repository returns local path.

title: Public datasets for individual electricity load forecasting
text: literature contains many public datasets useful benchmarking load forecasting models. recent survey Baur et al. provides structured list 25 open datasets electric load forecasting focusing transparency reproducibility. examples directly used elcf4R might relevant : UK-DALE: domestic appliance level electricity demand 16 kHz 1 second sampling UK homes, useful non intrusve load monitoring. ENERTALK: 15 Hz electricity consumption 22 Korean homes. REFIT: whole house submetered appliance loads 8 second resolution UK households. Synthetic French residential curves: recently released synthetic dataset French individual load curves generated latent diffusion model, attractive privacy preserving experiments. using datasets, always check licence citation requirements ensure intended use complies .

title: Public datasets for individual electricity load forecasting
text: make examples vignettes reproducible without large downloads, elcf4R currently ships following compact datasets: elcf4r_elmas_toy: toy subset ELMAS 18-cluster hourly profiles. elcf4r_iflex_example: compact hourly household-level panel iFlex temperature price-signal metadata. elcf4r_iflex_benchmark_index: index complete participant-days iFlex hourly panel. elcf4r_iflex_benchmark_results: saved rolling-origin benchmark results current GAM, MARS, KWF LSTM wrappers fixed iFlex cohort. dataset help page details variables provenance.

title: Public datasets for individual electricity load forecasting
text: serious benchmarking, recommended workflow : Use helper download functions obtain full original datasets machine. Use preprocessing functions elcf4R create clean daily load segments, join weather data build panel data frames suitable KWF, GAM, MARS LSTM models. Run forecasting functions rolling origin evaluation scheme compute NMAE, NRMSE, MASE sMAPE using elcf4r_metrics(). Store resulting scores plots together exact dataset versions ensure reproducibility. shipped object elcf4r_iflex_benchmark_results illustrates pattern fixed iFlex cohort. remainder package documentation describes steps detail.

title: iFlex Workflow with Shipped Example Data
text: vignette shows complete lightweight workflow using datasets ship package: elcf4r_iflex_example preprocessing model fitting examples. elcf4r_iflex_benchmark_results inspecting saved benchmark results.

title: iFlex Workflow with Shipped Example Data
iFlex Workflow with Shipped Example Data
text: work one participant keep example compact.
code: id1 <- subset(   elcf4r_iflex_example,   entity_id == unique(elcf4r_iflex_example$entity_id)[1] )  daily <- elcf4r_build_daily_segments(   id1,   carry_cols = c("participation_phase", "price_signal") )  dim(daily$segments) #> [1] 14 24 head(daily$covariates[, c("date", "dow", "temp_mean", "participation_phase")]) #>                         date       dow temp_mean participation_phase #> Exp_1__2020-01-06 2020-01-06    Monday  7.508333             Phase_1 #> Exp_1__2020-01-07 2020-01-07   Tuesday  7.254167             Phase_1 #> Exp_1__2020-01-08 2020-01-08 Wednesday  6.312500             Phase_1 #> Exp_1__2020-01-09 2020-01-09  Thursday  3.420833             Phase_1 #> Exp_1__2020-01-10 2020-01-10    Friday  1.445833             Phase_1 #> Exp_1__2020-01-11 2020-01-11  Saturday  6.754167             Phase_1

what: Fit forecasting models on the example panel
title: iFlex Workflow with Shipped Example Data
text: train first 10 days predict 11th day. LSTM example available keras3 tensorflow packages installed configured:
code: train_days <- daily$covariates$date[1:10] test_day <- daily$covariates$date[11]  train_long <- subset(id1, date %in% train_days) test_long <- subset(id1, date == test_day)  fit_gam <- elcf4r_fit_gam(   train_long[, c("y", "time_index", "dow", "month", "temp")],   use_temperature = TRUE ) pred_gam <- predict(   fit_gam,   newdata = test_long[, c("y", "time_index", "dow", "month", "temp")] )  fit_mars <- elcf4r_fit_mars(   train_long[, c("y", "time_index", "dow", "month", "temp")],   use_temperature = TRUE ) pred_mars <- predict(   fit_mars,   newdata = test_long[, c("y", "time_index", "dow", "month", "temp")] )  fit_kwf <- elcf4r_fit_kwf(   segments = daily$segments[1:10, ],   covariates = daily$covariates[1:10, , drop = FALSE],   target_covariates = daily$covariates[11, , drop = FALSE] ) pred_kwf <- predict(fit_kwf)  fit_kwf_clustered <- elcf4r_fit_kwf_clustered(   segments = daily$segments[1:10, ],   covariates = daily$covariates[1:10, , drop = FALSE],   target_covariates = daily$covariates[11, , drop = FALSE] ) pred_kwf_clustered <- predict(fit_kwf_clustered)  naive_day <- as.numeric(daily$segments[10, ])  rbind(   gam = unlist(elcf4r_metrics(test_long$y, pred_gam, naive_pred = naive_day)),   mars = unlist(elcf4r_metrics(test_long$y, pred_mars, naive_pred = naive_day)),   kwf = unlist(elcf4r_metrics(test_long$y, pred_kwf, naive_pred = naive_day)),   kwf_clustered = unlist(elcf4r_metrics(test_long$y, pred_kwf_clustered, naive_pred = naive_day)) ) #>                    nmae     nrmse     smape      mase #> gam           0.1961463 0.2355394 0.1796715 0.9274129 #> mars          0.1690549 0.1920249 0.1600477 0.7993201 #> kwf           0.1404839 0.1821267 0.1394477 0.6642319 #> kwf_clustered 0.1640983 0.1907997 0.1529166 0.7758848 fit_lstm <- elcf4r_fit_lstm(   segments = daily$segments[1:10, ],   covariates = daily$covariates[1:10, , drop = FALSE],   use_temperature = TRUE,   epochs = 1,   units = 4,   batch_size = 2,   verbose = 0 )  pred_lstm <- predict(fit_lstm) unlist(elcf4r_metrics(test_long$y, pred_lstm, naive_pred = naive_day)) #>      nmae     nrmse     smape      mase  #> 0.2215960 0.2522648 0.2061798 1.0477434 "LSTM example skipped because a working Keras/TensorFlow backend is not available in this R environment."

title: iFlex Workflow with Shipped Example Data

iflex_hourly_10_ids_28_train_5_test_5_methods   iflex     Exp_1           gam #>    test_date train_start  train_end train_days test_points use_temperature #> 1 2020-02-03  2020-01-06 2020-02-02 2020-02-03  2020-01-06 2020-02-02         28          24            TRUE #> 5 2020-02-03  2020-01-06 2020-02-02         28          24            TRUE #> 6 2020-02-04  2020-01-07 2020-02-03         28          24            TRUE #>   thermosensitive     thermosensitivity_status thermosensitivity_ratio #> 1              NA insufficient_summer_coverage                      NA #> 2              NA insufficient_summer_coverage                      NA #> 3              NA insufficient_summer_coverage                      NA #> 4              NA insufficient_summer_coverage                      NA #> 5              NA insufficient_summer_coverage                      NA #> 6              NA insufficient_summer_coverage                      NA #>   fit_seconds status error_message      nmae     nrmse      smape      mase #> 1       0.057     ok          <NA> 0.2241390 0.3029546 0.11050062 0.7570273 #> 2       0.037     ok          <NA> 0.2986949 0.3565707 0.15316209 1.0088391 #> 3       0.065     ok          <NA> 0.2622522 0.3346779 0.13014701 0.8857544 #> 4       0.142     ok          <NA> 0.2039969 0.2643067 0.09952173 0.6889975 #> 5       1.203     ok          <NA> 0.3218673 0.4079799 0.16103600 1.0871037 #> 6       0.031     ok          <NA> 0.1502679 0.2054841 0.12878440 0.8595982  aggregate(   cbind(nmae, nrmse, smape, mase, fit_seconds) ~ method,   data = elcf4r_iflex_benchmark_results,   FUN = function(x) round(mean(x, na.rm = TRUE), 4) ) #>          method   nmae  nrmse  smape   mase fit_seconds #> 1           gam 0.2435 0.3121 0.3222 0.8782      0.0250 #> 2           kwf 0.2740 0.3479 0.3477 0.9756      0.0379 #> 3 kwf_clustered 0.2640 0.3388 0.3295 0.9115      0.0904 #> 4          lstm 0.2296 0.2919 0.3188 0.8538      1.1791 #> 5          mars 0.2319 0.2946 0.3092 0.8310      0.0142

what: Frédéric Bertrand, Fatima Fahs and Myriam Maumy
title: Electricity Load Curves Forecasting at Individual Level
text: Implements Kernel Wavelet Functional (KWF), Generalized Additive Models (GAM), Multivariate Adaptive Regression Splines (MARS) RNN LSTM models forecast individual electricity load curves, following methodology described Fahs (2023) related articles posters. Includes small demo datasets helper functions download large public datasets ELMAS, StoreNet Ireland IDEAL. site created F. Bertrand examples reproduced created F. Bertrand, F. Fahs M. Maumy.

title: Electricity Load Curves Forecasting at Individual Level
text: can install latest version eclf4R package github :
code: devtools::install_github("fbertran/eclf4R")

title: Electricity Load Curves Forecasting at Individual Level
text: shipped iFlex benchmark now includes gam, mars, kwf lstm results fixed rolling-origin design.
code: aggregate(   cbind(nmae, nrmse, smape, fit_seconds) ~ method,   data = elcf4r_iflex_benchmark_results,   FUN = function(x) round(mean(x, na.rm = TRUE), 4) ) #>   method   nmae  nrmse  smape fit_seconds #> 1    gam 0.2435 0.3121 0.3222      0.0269 #> 2    kwf 0.2224 0.2861 0.2946      0.0007 #> 3   lstm 0.2296 0.2919 0.3188      1.1992 #> 4   mars 0.2319 0.2946 0.3092      0.0145

what: Build daily load-curve segments from a normalized panel — elcf4r_build_daily_segments
title: Build daily load-curve segments from a normalized panel — elcf4r_build_daily_segments
text: Convert long-format load table one row per entity-day one column per within-day time index. matrix representation required functional load-curve models rolling benchmark scripts.

title: Build daily load-curve segments from a normalized panel — elcf4r_build_daily_segments
code: elcf4r_build_daily_segments(   data,   id_col = "entity_id",   timestamp_col = "timestamp",   value_col = "y",   temp_col = "temp",   carry_cols = NULL,   expected_points_per_day = NULL,   resolution_minutes = NULL,   complete_days_only = TRUE,   drop_na_value = TRUE,   tz = "UTC" )

title: Build daily load-curve segments from a normalized panel — elcf4r_build_daily_segments
text: data Data frame containing least entity id, timestamp load. id_col Name entity identifier column. timestamp_col Name timestamp column. value_col Name load column. temp_col Optional name temperature column used derive day summaries. carry_cols Optional day-level columns propagate returned covariate table. first non-missing value within day kept. expected_points_per_day Expected number samples per day. NULL, derived resolution_minutes. resolution_minutes Sampling resolution minutes. NULL, inferred timestamps resolution_minutes column. Fractional minute values allowed. complete_days_only TRUE, incomplete duplicated days dropped output. drop_na_value TRUE, days missing load values dropped. tz Time zone used derive dates within-day positions.

title: Build daily load-curve segments from a normalized panel — elcf4r_build_daily_segments
text: list components segments, covariates, resolution_minutes points_per_day.

title: Build daily load-curve segments from a normalized panel — elcf4r_build_daily_segments
code: id1 <- subset(   elcf4r_iflex_example,   entity_id == unique(elcf4r_iflex_example$entity_id)[1] ) daily <- elcf4r_build_daily_segments(id1, carry_cols = "participation_phase") dim(daily$segments) #> [1] 14 24 names(daily$covariates) #>  [1] "day_key"             "entity_id"           "date"                #>  [4] "n_points"            "n_unique_index"      "has_duplicate_index" #>  [7] "has_missing_value"   "dow"                 "month"               #> [10] "temp_mean"           "temp_min"            "temp_max"            #> [13] "participation_phase"

title: Classify thermosensitivity from daily load data — elcf4r_classify_thermosensitivity
code: elcf4r_classify_thermosensitivity(   data,   id_col = "entity_id",   date_col = "date",   value_col = "y",   threshold = 1.5,   winter_months = c(12L, 1L, 2L),   summer_months = c(6L, 7L, 8L) )

title: Classify thermosensitivity from daily load data — elcf4r_classify_thermosensitivity
text: data Data frame containing least identifier, date load column. Long-format panels accepted aggregated mean daily load classification. id_col Name entity identifier column. date_col Name date column. value_col Name load column. threshold Ratio threshold series classified thermosensitive. Defaults 1.5. winter_months Integer vector winter months. summer_months Integer vector summer months.

title: Classify thermosensitivity from daily load data — elcf4r_classify_thermosensitivity
text: data frame one row per entity columns winter_mean, summer_mean, ratio, thermosensitive, status.

title: Classify thermosensitivity from daily load data — elcf4r_classify_thermosensitivity
code: example_ts <- data.frame(   entity_id = rep("home_1", 4),   date = as.Date(c("2024-01-10", "2024-01-11", "2024-07-10", "2024-07-11")),   y = c(12, 11, 6, 5) ) elcf4r_classify_thermosensitivity(example_ts) #>   entity_id winter_mean summer_mean    ratio threshold thermosensitive status #> 1    home_1        11.5         5.5 2.090909       1.5            TRUE     ok #>   n_winter_days n_summer_days #> 1             2             2

title: Fit a GAM model for load curves — elcf4r_fit_gam
code: elcf4r_fit_gam(data, use_temperature = FALSE)

title: Fit a GAM model for load curves — elcf4r_fit_gam
text: data Data frame columns y (load), time_index (numeric factor within day position), dow, month, optional temp covariates. use_temperature Logical. TRUE, include temperature smooth effect interactions.

title: Fit a GAM model for load curves — elcf4r_fit_gam
text: object class elcf4r_model method = "gam".

title: Fit a GAM model for load curves — elcf4r_fit_gam
code: id1 <- subset(   elcf4r_iflex_example,   entity_id == unique(elcf4r_iflex_example$entity_id)[1] ) train_data <- subset(id1, date < sort(unique(id1$date))[11]) test_data <- subset(id1, date == sort(unique(id1$date))[11]) fit <- elcf4r_fit_gam(train_data[, c("y", "time_index", "dow", "month", "temp")], TRUE) pred <- predict(fit, newdata = test_data[, c("y", "time_index", "dow", "month", "temp")]) length(pred) #> [1] 24

what: Fit a Kernel Wavelet Functional model for daily load curves — elcf4r_fit_kwf
title: Fit a Kernel Wavelet Functional model for daily load curves — elcf4r_fit_kwf
text: Fit day-ahead Kernel Wavelet Functional (KWF) model ordered daily load curves. implementation computes wavelet-detail distances historical context days, applies Gaussian kernel weights, restricts weights matching calendar groups available, can apply approximation/detail correction used mean-level non-stationarity.

title: Fit a Kernel Wavelet Functional model for daily load curves — elcf4r_fit_kwf
code: elcf4r_fit_kwf(   segments,   covariates = NULL,   target_covariates = NULL,   use_temperature = FALSE,   wavelet = "la12",   bandwidth = NULL,   use_mean_correction = TRUE,   group_col = NULL,   holidays = NULL,   weights = NULL,   recency_decay = NULL,   temperature_bandwidth = NULL )

title: Fit a Kernel Wavelet Functional model for daily load curves — elcf4r_fit_kwf
text: segments Matrix data frame past daily load curves (rows days, columns within-day time points) chronological order. covariates Optional data frame one row per training segment. present, function looks deterministic grouping information context_group, kwf_group, calendar_group, column named group_col. explicit group column present, groups derived date holidays, dow fallback. target_covariates Optional one-row data frame describing day forecast. contains date, previous day used context day calendar grouping, matches residential KWF protocol pre-holiday handling. use_temperature Deprecated ignored. Kept backward compatibility earlier package examples. wavelet Wavelet filter name passed wavelets::dwt(). Defaults "la12", least-asymmetric filter aligned thesis implementation. series short requested filter, function falls back "haar". bandwidth Optional positive bandwidth Gaussian kernel wavelet distances. NULL, inferred distances last observed segment. use_mean_correction Logical; TRUE, apply approximation/detail correction used mean-level non-stationarity. group_col Optional column name containing precomputed KWF groups covariates target_covariates. holidays Optional vector holiday dates used elcf4r_calendar_groups() deterministic groups derived date. weights Optional numeric prior weights length nrow(segments). first nrow(segments) - 1 values used historical pairing step. recency_decay Optional non-negative recency coefficient applied exponential prior historical context days. temperature_bandwidth Deprecated ignored. Kept backward compatibility older examples.

title: Fit a Kernel Wavelet Functional model for daily load curves — elcf4r_fit_kwf
text: object class elcf4r_model method = "kwf".

title: Fit a Kernel Wavelet Functional model for daily load curves — elcf4r_fit_kwf
code: id1 <- subset(   elcf4r_iflex_example,   entity_id == unique(elcf4r_iflex_example$entity_id)[1] ) daily <- elcf4r_build_daily_segments(id1, carry_cols = "participation_phase") fit <- elcf4r_fit_kwf(   segments = daily$segments[1:10, ],   covariates = daily$covariates[1:10, ],   target_covariates = daily$covariates[11, , drop = FALSE] ) length(predict(fit)) #> [1] 24

what: Fit a clustered KWF model for daily load curves — elcf4r_fit_kwf_clustered
title: Fit a clustered KWF model for daily load curves — elcf4r_fit_kwf_clustered
text: Cluster dyadically resampled daily curves wavelet-energy feature space use resulting cluster labels grouping structure inside KWF forecast.

title: Fit a clustered KWF model for daily load curves — elcf4r_fit_kwf_clustered
code: elcf4r_fit_kwf_clustered(   segments,   covariates = NULL,   target_covariates = NULL,   wavelet = "la12",   bandwidth = NULL,   use_mean_correction = TRUE,   max_clusters = 10L,   nstart = 30L,   cluster_seed = 1L,   weights = NULL,   recency_decay = NULL,   clustering = NULL )

title: Fit a clustered KWF model for daily load curves — elcf4r_fit_kwf_clustered
text: segments Matrix data frame past daily load curves chronological order. covariates Optional data frame one row per segment. target_covariates Optional one-row data frame target day. wavelet Wavelet filter name passed wavelets::dwt(). Defaults "la12". bandwidth Optional positive bandwidth Gaussian kernel underlying KWF fit. use_mean_correction Logical; TRUE, apply approximation/detail correction underlying KWF fit. max_clusters Maximum number candidate clusters considered Sugar jump heuristic. nstart Number random starts kmeans. cluster_seed Optional integer seed used make clustering deterministic benchmark use. weights Optional prior weights passed base KWF fit. recency_decay Optional recency prior passed base KWF fit. clustering Optional elcf4r_kwf_clusters object. supplied, stored clustering model reused instead refit.

title: Fit a clustered KWF model for daily load curves — elcf4r_fit_kwf_clustered
text: object class elcf4r_model method = "kwf_clustered".

what: Fit an LSTM model for daily load curves — elcf4r_fit_lstm
title: Fit an LSTM model for daily load curves — elcf4r_fit_lstm
text: LSTM implementation uses one previous daily curves predict next daily curve. use_temperature = TRUE temp_mean available covariates, daily mean temperature added second input feature repeated across within-day time steps.

title: Fit an LSTM model for daily load curves — elcf4r_fit_lstm
code: elcf4r_fit_lstm(   segments,   covariates = NULL,   use_temperature = FALSE,   lookback_days = 1L,   units = 16L,   epochs = 10L,   batch_size = 8L,   validation_split = 0,   seed = 123L,   verbose = 0L )

title: Fit an LSTM model for daily load curves — elcf4r_fit_lstm
text: segments Matrix data frame past daily load curves (rows days, columns time points). covariates Optional data frame one row per training day. use_temperature Logical. TRUE, use temp_mean covariates additional input feature available. lookback_days Number past daily curves used one training input. units Number hidden units LSTM layer. epochs Number training epochs. batch_size Batch size used keras3::fit(). validation_split Validation split passed keras3::fit(). seed Random seed passed TensorFlow. verbose Verbosity level passed keras3::fit() predict().

title: Fit an LSTM model for daily load curves — elcf4r_fit_lstm
text: object class elcf4r_model method = "lstm".

title: Fit an LSTM model for daily load curves — elcf4r_fit_lstm
code: if (interactive() &&     requireNamespace("keras3", quietly = TRUE) &&     requireNamespace("tensorflow", quietly = TRUE) &&     suppressWarnings(       tryCatch(         utils::getFromNamespace("is_keras_available", "keras3")(),         error = function(e) FALSE       )     )) {   id1 <- subset(     elcf4r_iflex_example,     entity_id == unique(elcf4r_iflex_example$entity_id)[1]   )   daily <- elcf4r_build_daily_segments(id1)   fit <- elcf4r_fit_lstm(     segments = daily$segments[1:10, ],     covariates = daily$covariates[1:10, ],     use_temperature = TRUE,     epochs = 1,     units = 4,     batch_size = 2,     verbose = 0   )   length(predict(fit)) }

what: iFlex benchmark index of complete participant-days — elcf4r_iflex_benchmark_index
title: iFlex benchmark index of complete participant-days — elcf4r_iflex_benchmark_index
text: compact index complete days derived public iFlex hourly panel. row represents one participant-day enough metadata define deterministic benchmark cohorts without shipping full raw panel.

title: iFlex benchmark index of complete participant-days — elcf4r_iflex_benchmark_index
text: data frame 563,150 rows 11 variables: day_key Unique key built entity_id__date. entity_id Participant identifier. date Calendar date. dow Day week. month Month two-digit factor. temp_mean Mean daily outdoor temperature degrees Celsius. temp_min Minimum daily outdoor temperature degrees Celsius. temp_max Maximum daily outdoor temperature degrees Celsius. participation_phase Experiment phase source dataset. price_signal Experimental price-signal label, available. n_points Number hourly samples retained day.

title: iFlex benchmark index of complete participant-days — elcf4r_iflex_benchmark_index
text: Public iFlex raw file data_hourly.csv, reduced data-raw/elcf4r_iflex_subsets.R.

what: iFlex benchmark results for shipped forecasting methods — elcf4r_iflex_benchmark_results
title: iFlex benchmark results for shipped forecasting methods — elcf4r_iflex_benchmark_results
text: Saved benchmark results deterministic rolling-origin evaluation subset iFlex data. shipped results use 10 participant IDs, 28-day training window 5 one-day test forecasts per participant. current shipped benchmark includes operational gam, mars, kwf, kwf_clustered lstm wrappers.

title: iFlex benchmark results for shipped forecasting methods — elcf4r_iflex_benchmark_results
text: data frame 250 rows 20 variables: benchmark_name Identifier benchmark design. dataset Dataset label, always "iflex". entity_id Participant identifier. method Forecasting method: gam, mars, kwf, kwf_clustered lstm. test_date Date forecast target day. train_start First day training window. train_end Last day training window. train_days Number training days. test_points Number hourly points target day. use_temperature Logical flag temperature-aware fitting. thermosensitive Thermosensitivity flag seasonal coverage sufficient, otherwise NA. thermosensitivity_status Status winter/summer ratio classification step. thermosensitivity_ratio Estimated winter/summer mean-load ratio available. fit_seconds Elapsed fit--predict time seconds. status Benchmark execution status. error_message Error message fit failed. nmae Normalized mean absolute error. nrmse Normalized root mean squared error. smape Symmetric mean absolute percentage error. mase Mean absolute scaled error.

title: iFlex benchmark results for shipped forecasting methods — elcf4r_iflex_benchmark_results
text: Derived elcf4r_iflex_benchmark_index public iFlex raw file data-raw/elcf4r_iflex_benchmark_results.R.

what: iFlex example panel for package examples — elcf4r_iflex_example
title: iFlex example panel for package examples — elcf4r_iflex_example
text: compact hourly electricity-demand panel extracted public iFlex dataset. object contains 14 complete days 3 participants intended examples, tests lightweight vignettes.

title: iFlex example panel for package examples — elcf4r_iflex_example
text: data frame 1,008 rows 16 variables: dataset Dataset label, always "iflex". entity_id Participant identifier. timestamp Hourly UTC timestamp. date Calendar date observation. time_index Within-day hourly index 1 24. y Hourly electricity demand kWh. temp Outdoor temperature degrees Celsius. dow Day week. month Month two-digit factor. resolution_minutes Sampling resolution minutes. participation_phase Experiment phase source dataset. price_signal Experimental price-signal label, available. price_nok_kwh Experimental electricity price NOK per kWh. temp24 Lagged 24-hour temperature feature source file. temp48 Lagged 48-hour temperature feature source file. temp72 Lagged 72-hour temperature feature source file.

title: iFlex example panel for package examples — elcf4r_iflex_example
text: Public iFlex raw file data_hourly.csv, reduced data-raw/elcf4r_iflex_subsets.R.

title: Forecast accuracy metrics for load curves — elcf4r_metrics
text: truth Numeric vector matrix observed values. pred Numeric vector matrix predicted values, shape. seasonal_period Seasonal period naive benchmark MASE denominator (daily curves half hourly sampling, value 48 appropriate). naive_pred Optional numeric vector matrix naive benchmark predictions shape truth. supplied, MASE computed explicit naive forecast instead inferring denominator seasonal_period within truth.

what: Normalize a load panel to the elcf4R schema — elcf4r_normalize_panel

title: Normalize a load panel to the elcf4R schema — elcf4r_normalize_panel
code: elcf4r_normalize_panel(   data,   id_col,   timestamp_col,   load_col,   temp_col = NULL,   dataset = NA_character_,   resolution_minutes = NULL,   tz = "UTC",   keep_cols = NULL )

title: Normalize a load panel to the elcf4R schema — elcf4r_normalize_panel
text: data Data frame containing least entity identifier, time stamp load column. id_col Name entity identifier column. timestamp_col Name timestamp column. load_col Name load column. temp_col Optional name temperature column. dataset Short dataset label stored normalized output. resolution_minutes Sampling resolution minutes. NULL, inferred timestamps. Fractional minute values allowed high-frequency data. tz Time zone used parse timestamps. keep_cols Optional character vector extra source columns keep.

title: Normalize a load panel to the elcf4R schema — elcf4r_normalize_panel
text: data frame normalized columns dataset, entity_id, timestamp, date, time_index, y, temp, dow, month resolution_minutes, plus requested keep_cols.

title: Read and normalize the iFlex hourly dataset — elcf4r_read_iflex
text: path Path data_hourly.csv directory contains . ids Optional vector participant identifiers keep. start Optional inclusive lower time bound. end Optional inclusive upper time bound. tz Time zone used parse timestamps. Defaults "UTC" iFlex timestamps stored trailing Z. n_max Optional maximum number rows read. Intended quick prototyping small subset raw file.

code: elcf4r_read_refit(   path = "data-raw",   house_ids = NULL,   channels = "Aggregate",   start = NULL,   end = NULL,   tz = "UTC",   resolution_minutes = 1L,   agg_fun = c("mean", "sum", "last"),   n_max = NULL,   drop_na_load = TRUE )

title: Read and normalize the REFIT cleaned household dataset — elcf4r_read_refit
text: path Path REFIT file directory containing CLEAN_House*.csv files. house_ids Optional vector house identifiers keep. matched file stems "CLEAN_House1". channels Character vector load channels extract. Defaults "Aggregate". start Optional inclusive lower time bound. end Optional inclusive upper time bound. tz Time zone used parse timestamps. resolution_minutes Target regular resolution minutes normalized output. Defaults 1. agg_fun Aggregation used resampling target grid. One "mean", "sum" "last". n_max Optional maximum number raw rows read per file. drop_na_load Logical; TRUE, rows missing load values dropped resampling.

title: Read and normalize the REFIT cleaned household dataset — elcf4r_read_refit
text: normalized data frame REFIT household data.

Defaults "Consumption(W)". keep_cols Optional extra source columns keep. Defaults main battery production fields present.
